paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces global-local attention with relative position encoding to handle long sequences efficiently. Uses a hybrid strategy combining global tokens and local sliding windows, allowing processing of sequences up to 4096 tokens without truncation while maintaining competitive performance
raw_extraction.parameters: 128M (estimated based on hidden layers and dimensions from Table 13/14/15)
raw_extraction.application: Long-document question answering, document classification, genomic sequence analysis, summarization
raw_extraction.license: null (likely proprietary/open-source details not specified in this excerpt)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Handling long sequences in transformer architectures for NLP tasks like question answering, summarization, and document classification
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Question Answering, Document Classification, GLUE Benchmark Tasks
raw_extraction.paper_title: Big Bird: Transformers for Longer Sequences

raw_extraction.innovation: Global-local attention with explicit designed attention connections for better handling of structured inputs. Uses global tokens for high-level context and local random tokens for fine-grained details, improving efficiency and performance across long sequences
raw_extraction.parameters: 128M (same architecture as ITC but optimized for ETC-style global/local attention)
raw_extraction.application: Long-document question answering, document classification, genomic sequence analysis, summarization, multi-document reasoning
raw_extraction.research_problem: Enabling transformer models to process long sequences effectively for various NLP applications
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: Question Answering, Document Classification, GLUE Benchmark Tasks, Summarization
